what: A Library of Awesome: a growing, static, agent-contributable catalog of buildable use-cases for the RAPP static-API stack.
how_agents_use_this: Match a need to a scenario via 'when_to_use', then read its data_url for the record and demo_url for the working page. Contribute new scenarios per llms.txt.

stack./track: QR -> hash-verified 'extract' cell runs in the scanner's browser, fetches a free live source, renders one value. No server.
stack./fn: verify-before-exec compute cells: fetch bytes, recompute SHA-256, refuse on mismatch, import exact bytes, run offscreen in a sandboxed iframe.
stack./vbrainstem-cell: an entire CPython 3.12 (Pyodide/WASM) boots headless in an opaque-origin sandboxed iframe. Zero install, no server, no tab.
stack./mcp: a static MCP server (Node stdio shim, zero per-tool logic; each tool = a pinned verified cell). Any LLM speaks to it with no browser.
stack./resolver: short-id ([owner/]name[/subpath][@sha8]) -> full constellation URL; the host lives in exactly one line.
stack.rapp-twin.profile.md: the two-sided digital twin (god on-device lead / dog public bones), sealed privacy, Dream Catcher merge — nothing is lost.
stack.loop.prompt.md: a paste-over-anything improvement ratchet that discovers its own score and keeps only verified wins.

channels.title: Physical World
channels.blurb: QR on paper, objects, stickers, skin, stone — the physical thing runs live code inside whoever looks at it.

channels.title: Verify-Before-Exec
channels.blurb: Tamper-evident, un-killable, trust-the-hash-not-the-host. Run untrusted-CDN code and prove it wasn't changed.

channels.title: Agent to Agent
channels.blurb: MCP tools, brainstems as bytecode, signed dead-drops — machines using the stack with no human and no browser.

channels.title: Human & Personal
channels.blurb: Twins, gifts, memorials, time capsules, dead-man switches — the emotional edge of a serverless, forever stack.

channels.title: Self-Building
channels.blurb: The loop, the tumbler, systems that improve and verify themselves — including this library.

channels.title: Wildcard
channels.blurb: The strange stacks that shouldn't work but provably do. Go weird, stay real.

channels.title: Play
channels.blurb: Games and toys — playable, no server: scan-to-join, pass-and-play, physics sandboxes, real-time fun.

channels.title: Art
channels.blurb: Generative and camera visuals — beauty seeded by a hash, live data, or your own face.

channels.title: Sound
channels.blurb: Instruments and sonification — the Web Audio API turned into synths, toys, and data you can hear.

channels.title: Social
channels.blurb: Collaborative, no backend — shared canvases, tanks, walls and stories built on GitHub Issues.

scenarios.title: Broadcast the Secret
scenarios.when_to_use: When data must be published to a fully public, permanent store yet remain readable only by a key-holder — privacy as a seal, not a location.

scenarios.title: Kill the Host
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need an endpoint that survives its origin being deleted — resolve+verify the same content from any mirror.

scenarios.title: One String, Two Universes
scenarios.when_to_use: When the same verified capability must be callable by an AI (MCP, no browser) and a human (QR, no install) with identical trust.

scenarios.title: Taste the Weather
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: live external data rendered client-side from a scannable/shareable trigger, with no backend and a tamper-proof compute step.

scenarios.title: The Byte Trap
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to run code fetched from an untrusted host and prove byte-for-byte it wasn't altered before it executes.

scenarios.title: The Enchanted Mirror
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a no-backend live webcam visual that feels magical, discloses its source artwork, and keeps all frame processing local.
scenarios.weld: Transplants taste-the-weather's narrated live #go flow and provenance-camera's canvas-first rendering discipline, then welds them into a webcam-to-brushstroke pipeline colored by a random museum artwork palette on each reload.

scenarios.title: The Quine Ratchet
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a domain-agnostic improvement loop that discovers its own score, keeps only verified gains, and stops itself at genuine human-decision boundaries.

scenarios.title: The Reunion Where Two Memories Fuse
scenarios.when_to_use: When two datasets must merge without ever losing or overwriting a record — conflicts preserved as alternate frames, append-only.

scenarios.title: The Verifiable Tournament
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a tamper-evident live ranking where every entry is cryptographically signed by the player's own browser key and the entire board is freshness-stamped so no stale screenshot can pass as current.
scenarios.weld: Transplant the-unbreakable-promise's ECDSA sign/verify loop to sign each player's tournament entry, proof-of-freshness's sha256Hex + arrayBuffer fetch to stamp the live ranking payload, and living-leaderboard's runVerifiedCell + parseTopRows to fetch and render the board — the signed player slot is injected into the freshness-stamped live board so every rank is either signed by its maker or provably current.

scenarios.title: Photosynthesis
scenarios.when_to_use: When a client-side artifact should both consume live data and emit a new shareable trigger, with zero backend.

scenarios.title: The Immortal Committer
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a permanent physical marker that renders a live, ever-updating public number with no server to maintain.

scenarios.title: The Paper That Boots Python
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a full language runtime on a device with nothing installed, delivered by a scannable trigger.

scenarios.title: Proof of Freshness
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to prove a displayed live value was fetched now (not staged) and bind it to a browser-verifiable hash of the exact response bytes.

scenarios.title: The Unbreakable Promise
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a tamper-evident personal commitment that anyone can re-verify offline from shared text + key + signature.

scenarios.title: Chain of Custody
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to prove the complete history of a document or artifact (not just current bytes): validate each version's hash and parent link from a trusted genesis hash, and fail loudly at the first broken hop.

scenarios.title: Living Leaderboard
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a physical trigger that always resolves to a fresh ranked list by fetching public data in-browser with verify-before-exec.

scenarios.title: The Menu That Knows the Kitchen
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a physical artifact to reflect live operational state without a backend: treat GitHub Issues as the CRUD store and map labels to state, while still verifying compute bytes before exec in the client.

scenarios.title: The Gravity Organ
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a no-backend interactive instrument/game where physics drives sound and mobile tilt changes behavior live.

scenarios.title: Kept Weather
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to turn live place weather into a persistent, shareable, no-backend living artifact.

scenarios.title: Nobody Drew This Alone
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: no-backend collaborative creation where each participant contributes a partial view and the final artifact is append-only shared state.
scenarios.weld: Uses The Tank's no-backend issue-backed shared state, then applies The Reunion's additive preserve-every-contribution logic so each blind segment permanently joins a stitched creature.

scenarios.title: Only in the Same Frame
scenarios.when_to_use: When a secret must have NO location until two independently-held carriers meet: XOR-split an AES-256 key into 2-of-2 shares, put {ciphertext + share A} on one carrier and {share B} on the other, and gate every read on physical co-presence — neither share alone leaks a bit, and a hash-verified /fn cell reassembles the key and decrypts entirely client-side, so no server ever holds a half or witnesses the join.

scenarios.title: The Spy at the Table
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: instant multi-player role dealing where each player privately reveals a secret from a static page without any backend.

scenarios.title: The Artifact With a Signed Bloodline
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need tamper-evident provenance / version history with no server or ledger: have each new state sign SHA-256(state) ‖ the previous signature, persist the append-only chain locally, and let any holder of the public key walk it to pinpoint the exact generation where the lineage breaks — and re-verify a shared head from a QR alone.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Autonomous Economy
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a zero-backend economy where sealed, custody-proven, seller-signed orders with verified-reputation sellers flow through a tamper-refusing autonomous agent pipeline and every stage — reputation, seal, custody, signature — is independently verifiable in the browser.
scenarios.weld: Run the full Trusted Exchange pipeline (live availability → AES-sealed order → SHA-256 custody → ECDSA receipt → signed reputation), then inject the complete notarized economic packet as the Sovereign Handoff relay genesis so every autonomous hop verifies custody and signature before forwarding.

scenarios.title: The Brainstem Baton
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: to hand a running agent's live state from one machine/process to another through a single static, content-addressed, sealed file — pause on host A, resume on host B from the exact step, with a hash proving the state wasn't altered in transit and a key gating who may read it.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Command Center
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need one zero-backend operator console that watches a sealed-and-signed economy run live while also verifying external feeds, so order intake, custody, reputation, and ambient signals stay provable on one screen.
scenarios.weld: Keep the entire autonomous economy flow intact, then surface its live order, custody, and reputation outputs beside the situation room's verified external-feed tiles so one operator console watches the notarized economy run in real time.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Democratic Market
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a no-backend economy where market operations are permanent and notarized, and governance/moderation decisions are made by verifiable append-only signed ballots instead of an admin.
scenarios.weld: Keep the full permanent market pipeline intact, but gate each order with a trustless poll where signed ballots race append-only consensus and the ratified governance decision is recorded permanently with the sale.

scenarios.title: The Far Window
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a quiet, location-specific live truth from a printed object, with verify-before-exec and no backend.

scenarios.title: The Fast-Forward Parliament
scenarios.when_to_use: When N autonomous agents must agree on one value (or a canonical order) with no coordinator, no quorum math, and no vote tally: model the decision as an atomic compare-and-swap on one shared ref (a git fast-forward-only push, or a native atomic transaction) — every agent bases on the same parent, exactly one write lands per parent, and the accepted ref IS the decision.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Heirloom
scenarios.when_to_use: When data must survive generations with verifiable chain-of-custody yet remain readable only by the current rightful holder: sign each new keyholder's public key with the prior generation's ECDSA key, seal the payload AES-256-GCM under the heir's passphrase, and publish both openly — anyone verifies the lineage, only the heir reads the contents.
scenarios.weld: Transplant the ECDSA generational chain from the-artifact-with-a-signed-bloodline (each generation's key signs the next's public key) and the AES-256-GCM RSL1 seal from broadcast-the-secret; weld point = the heir at the tip of the verified bloodline is the one whose passphrase opens the seal.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Immortal Ledger
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a no-backend ledger whose entries can be fetched from any surviving mirror, whose full ancestry is tamper-evident, and whose newest head can prove it was minted from live bytes right now.
scenarios.weld: Resolve the same pinned cell across dead and surviving mirrors, mint each ledger entry from a fresh byte-hash proof, then chain those entries together so the whole mirror-surviving history re-verifies from genesis to head.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Live Situation Room
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need one scannable physical dashboard that fuses multiple live public signals into a single tamper-evident operational view.
scenarios.weld: Transplants the donors' full weather, repo-star heartbeat, and markdown leaderboard verify-before-exec flows into one live triage wall where each tile verifies before it fetches.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Living Spec
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a living specification that can prove its own bytes and continuously generate the next executable conformance test from its current rules, with no backend.
scenarios.weld: The page first attests its own index.html hash, then feeds that attested rule text into a CPython fuzzer that shrinks a failing counterexample and emits a pinned child conformance test QR that re-runs verify-before-exec.

scenarios.title: The Night You Were Born
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need an emotional, no-backend visualization where a personal input (date/place) deterministically recomputes a real physical state in-browser.
scenarios.weld: Transplants Taste the Weather's quiet single-panel restraint and Gravity Organ's canvas animation, then welds deterministic astronomy math so date+longitude alone reconstructs a real sky with moon phase.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Notarized Marketplace
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a zero-backend marketplace where inventory is live, orders stay sealed, custody is tamper-evident, and every completion emits a seller-signed receipt buyers can verify independently.
scenarios.weld: Keep the Sealed Storefront flow intact, then sign each completed custody-stamped order with an ECDSA seller key so every buyer gets a cryptographically verifiable receipt bound to the exact sealed envelope and custody head.

scenarios.title: The Page That Proves Itself
scenarios.when_to_use: When you (an agent) need: a client-side, zero-dependency tamper-evident attestation that the artifact being served is byte-for-byte identical to what was originally published.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Permanent Market
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a zero-backend market where inventory is live, orders stay sealed, receipts are seller-signed, and every completed transaction is preserved in a mirror-surviving append-only ledger with browser-side freshness and lineage proofs.
scenarios.weld: Keep the full notarized marketplace flow intact, then append every completed seller-signed receipt as a fresh-proof ledger entry whose whole history re-verifies from a mirror-surviving genesis to the current head.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Provable Raffle
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a raffle where tickets are unforgeable and committed before the draw, the draw entropy is provably fresh (SHA-256 of live public bytes) and unriggable (future drand randomness), and any browser reruns the same deterministic selection — no trusted operator needed.
scenarios.weld: The draw seed is the SHA-256 of live public bytes (freshness proof) combined with a future drand round's randomness, so the winner is unriggable, provably can't be known before the draw, can't be backdated, and only valid committed tickets are eligible.

scenarios.title: The Provenance Camera
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to stamp a captured datum with a tamper-evident genesis hash, build a verifiable custody chain, and emit a scannable QR pedigree — all client-side, no server.
scenarios.weld: chain-of-custody's sha256 genesis + parent-link verification stamps the captured datum; photosynthesis's qr.mjs draws the resulting head hash onto a canvas — the chain's output is the QR's input.

scenarios.title: The Relay
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a multi-stage agent pipeline where each stage must prove the prior stage's output was not tampered with before acting on it — model each handoff as a content-addressed frame and verify the SHA-256 before every transform.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Sealed Ballot
scenarios.when_to_use: When agents need a true secret ballot without trusted counters: sign canonical votes per key, seal each vote at cast time, append sealed envelopes by fast-forward consensus, then unseal at close to verify one valid vote per distinct key.
scenarios.weld: Keep Trustless Poll's signed-ballot fast-forward race intact, but cast each signed ballot only as a Broadcast-the-Secret RSL1 envelope and reveal+verify signatures after close.

scenarios.title: The Sealed Storefront
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a zero-backend storefront with live inventory gating, sealed orders readable only by the seller, and tamper-evident custody of the sealed order envelope from submission through delivery.
scenarios.weld: IssuesDB availability gate → AES-256-GCM seals the order payload into an RSL1 envelope → SHA-256 content-addresses that envelope into a custody chain link, so the order's journey is both confidential and tamper-evident end-to-end

scenarios.title: WELD: The Self-Notarizing Record
scenarios.when_to_use: When you (an agent) need: an editable record that can prove its current bytes still match the latest published head and that every prior revision still descends from a trusted genesis hash.
scenarios.weld: Keep the live self-hash attestation intact, keep the full parent-linked custody replay intact, and weld them by appending each freshly computed self-hash as the next version link whenever the record is saved.

scenarios.title: The Self-Proving Relay
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a relay where every hop must prove both inbound integrity and local runtime integrity, and must halt immediately if either proof fails.
scenarios.weld: Each stage first verifies the prior content-addressed frame, then fetches+hashes this page to attest self-integrity, then runs through a verify-before-exec code gate that refuses cold on any byte mismatch before emitting the next frame.

scenarios.title: The Sound of Far Away
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a restrained no-backend sonification page where live external conditions become a continuous ambient presence, not a numeric dashboard.
scenarios.weld: Transplants taste-the-weather's verify-before-exec live fetch pattern and gravity-organ's browser-only Web Audio canvas loop, then welds them so real coastal wind/wave values shape a quiet ambient soundscape.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Sovereign Handoff
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a chain of autonomous agents to hand off a high-value order through untrusted hops while preserving and proving seal confidentiality, custody continuity, and seller signature authenticity end-to-end.
scenarios.weld: Transplant the full notarized marketplace order pipeline as the relay payload, then force each self-proving relay hop to verify inbound frame bytes, attest its own source, and halt on any mismatch before forwarding custody-stamped signed bytes.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Sovereign Ticket
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need an in-person credential that must be issuer-authentic, unpredictably redeemable, and physically co-present at the moment of use: sign the exact ticket payload, commit its redeem proof to a future public randomness beacon, and split the decryption key across the holder and venue so neither side alone can spend it.
scenarios.weld: Sign the exact ticket payload with the promise donor, bind its single-use redeem proof to a future drand round with the unriggable donor, then seal that signed packet behind the same-frame donor's 2-of-2 XOR key shares so the door can spend it only when holder and venue meet in person.

scenarios.title: The Tank
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a no-backend shared toy where everyone contributes persistent objects through issue-backed state.

scenarios.title: The Test That Breeds
scenarios.when_to_use: When a client-side artifact should discover a fact by running code (a property-based fuzzer that finds + minimizes a counterexample), freeze that finding into a content-addressed, self-contained payload, and emit a new scannable trigger that re-verifies and re-executes the exact finding on any other device — a compute cell whose output is the next compute cell, with no backend.

scenarios.title: The Trailhead
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need one calm, place-specific safety readout from live data, delivered from a physical marker with no backend.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Trusted Exchange
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a zero-backend exchange where buying from a seller emits a custody-proven signed receipt and immediately updates that seller on a cryptographically signed, provably-fresh reputation ranking.
scenarios.weld: Keep the full notarized order pipeline intact, then pipe each completed seller-signed receipt into the tournament's sign-plus-freshness leaderboard so seller reputation updates are both attributable and provably live.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Trustless Poll
scenarios.when_to_use: When autonomous agents must produce a tally that nobody can forge, retract, or double-cast: have each agent sign one canonical ballot with its own browser key, then race those signed ballots through one append-only fast-forward ref so the consensus ledger itself becomes the verifiable poll result.
scenarios.weld: Keep the promise donor's exact ECDSA ballot sign-and-verify loop intact, feed each signed receipt into the parliament donor's append-only fast-forward race, and treat the resulting verified ledger as the poll tally itself.

scenarios.title: The Unriggable Ticket
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a tamper-proof outcome that no party can bias or predict and no server hosts: commit the inputs up front (printed / in a link), bind the result to a FUTURE public randomness beacon (drand League of Entropy), and let the verifier recompute it byte-for-byte in their own browser through a hash-pinned cell. Reusable for provably-fair draws, unriggable raffles, commit-reveal, fair coin-flips, and any 'trust me' that should be 'verify me instead'.

scenarios.title: WELD: The Verifiable Broadcast
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a live public feed that survives origin deletion and proves every emission was fetched now, not replayed from a stale snapshot.
scenarios.weld: Each mirror-resolved broadcast value is immediately stamped with a fetch-timestamp and SHA-256 of the exact response bytes, so a silenced host cannot prevent broadcast and a replayed stale value cannot disguise its age.
